scripts.title: Network snapshot (IP, gateway, DNS)
scripts.summary: Print current network settings for troubleshooting without changing anything.
steps: Run script as normal user.
Review interface addresses, default route, and DNS output.
Save output before making network changes.
expected_output: Hostname, interface list, default route, and DNS server lines.
scripts.rollback: No rollback required. Script is read-only.
notes: Use this first before and after network changes to compare state.

scripts.title: Set static IP with NetworkManager (nmcli)
scripts.summary: Switch a NetworkManager connection to static IPv4 with gateway and DNS.
prerequisites: You have console access in case network drops
steps: Run the script and choose an existing connection name.
Enter static IP/CIDR, gateway, and DNS servers.
Verify connectivity with ping after script completes.
expected_output: Connection profile shows static IPv4 address and DNS values.
scripts.rollback: Use the DHCP revert script in this wiki for the same connection.
notes: Wrong values can disconnect the machine.
Best practice: test on non-critical systems first.
script_lines: sudo nmcli connection modify "$CONN" ipv4.method manual ipv4.addresses "$IP_CIDR" ipv4.gateway "$GATEWAY" ipv4.dns "$DNS"

scripts.title: Revert NetworkManager connection to DHCP
scripts.summary: Set an nmcli connection back to automatic DHCP settings.
steps: Run script and pick connection previously modified.
Script switches ipv4.method to auto and removes manual values.
Reconnect and verify new DHCP address.
expected_output: Connection shows new DHCP-assigned IPv4 address.
scripts.rollback: Reapply a known-good static profile if DHCP does not work.
notes: Useful as immediate rollback after bad static IP configuration.
script_lines: sudo nmcli connection modify "$CONN" ipv4.method auto ipv4.addresses "" ipv4.gateway "" ipv4.dns ""

scripts.title: Change system hostname
scripts.summary: Safely set a new persistent hostname with hostnamectl.
steps: Run script and enter new hostname (letters, numbers, hyphens).
Script applies hostname and prints result.
Re-login or reboot if tools still show old hostname.
expected_output: hostnamectl output shows updated static hostname.
scripts.rollback: Run script again and set previous hostname value.
notes: Changing hostname can affect monitoring and inventory tools.
script_lines: echo "Hostname updated. You may need to re-login."

scripts.title: Fix folder ownership recursively
scripts.summary: Recursively set user and group owner for a directory tree.
steps: Provide target directory, user, and group.
Script runs chown -R and prints resulting directory owner.
Verify application behavior after ownership update.
expected_output: Top directory shows requested owner and group.
scripts.rollback: Run again with previous owner:group values if needed.
notes: Incorrect ownership can break apps and services.
Avoid using this on system directories unless required.

scripts.title: Normalize permissions (dirs 755, files 644)
scripts.summary: Set common safe defaults: directories 755, files 644.
prerequisites: You own target files or have sufficient privileges
steps: Enter target directory.
Script updates directory and file modes recursively.
Reapply execute permissions to scripts/binaries when needed.
expected_output: Confirmation message after find/chmod commands complete.
scripts.rollback: Restore from backup or manually reset modes for special files.
notes: Some applications require custom permissions.
Not recommended for executable bin folders unless you review first.

scripts.title: Make a script executable
scripts.summary: Apply execute permission to a shell script and show result.
steps: Enter script path.
Script runs chmod +x and prints file permissions.
expected_output: File mode includes executable bit (x).
scripts.rollback: Run chmod -x <script> if execute bit should be removed.
notes: This is one of the most common beginner fixes for 'permission denied'.

scripts.title: Create new user and grant admin group
scripts.summary: Create user account and add to wheel/sudo admin group.
steps: Enter a new username.
Set user password when prompted by passwd.
Script adds user to wheel or sudo group if present.
expected_output: id output shows created user and group memberships.
scripts.rollback: Use userdel -r <username> only if you are sure account should be removed.
notes: Never remove your only working admin account until new account is verified.

scripts.title: Reset a user's password
scripts.summary: Reset password for an existing account using passwd.
steps: Enter target username.
Type a new password when passwd prompts.
Inform user to test login immediately.
expected_output: passwd prints success message after update.
scripts.rollback: Run script again to set a different password if needed.
notes: For forgotten root password from boot/recovery mode, follow distro-specific rescue docs.

scripts.title: Force password change on next login
scripts.summary: Expire current password so user must change it at next login.
steps: Enter target username.
Script sets password last-change date to 0.
Review policy output from chage -l.
expected_output: chage report indicates password must be changed.
scripts.rollback: Set a new password with passwd and adjust aging policy with chage if needed.
notes: Useful after temporary password resets.

scripts.title: Check service health quickly
scripts.summary: Show active/enabled status and recent logs for a systemd service.
steps: Enter service name (example: sshd, nginx, docker).
Review active/enabled state and last log lines.
expected_output: Service state and recent journal entries.
scripts.rollback: No rollback needed; this script reads status only.
notes: If service name is unknown, run: systemctl list-unit-files --type=service

scripts.title: Restart service with confirmation
scripts.summary: Show current status, ask for confirmation, then restart a service.
prerequisites: You know expected service behavior after restart
steps: Enter service name.
Review current status preview.
Type yes to confirm restart.
expected_output: Final status output reflects service after restart attempt.
scripts.rollback: Use systemctl start/stop/restart with known-good configuration backups.
notes: Restarting production services can cause user-visible downtime.

scripts.title: Audit disk usage and large /var folders
scripts.summary: Display filesystem usage and largest top-level directories under /var.
steps: Run script and inspect full filesystem usage.
Check large /var paths before cleanup decisions.
expected_output: df table and size-sorted /var directory list.
scripts.rollback: No rollback needed; script is read-only.
notes: Use this before deleting logs/cache to target biggest space consumers.

scripts.title: Clean old systemd journal logs
scripts.summary: Vacuum journal logs older than specified days to recover disk space.
steps: Review current journal disk usage shown by script.
Enter retention days (example: 7).
Script vacuums old logs and prints new usage.
expected_output: journalctl reports reduced disk usage after vacuum.
scripts.rollback: Old logs removed by vacuum cannot be restored unless backed up.
notes: Keep enough days for troubleshooting and audit needs.
script_lines: read -rp "Keep how many days of logs? (example 7): " DAYS

scripts.title: Show recent critical system errors
scripts.summary: Show priority 3 (errors) from current boot and list failed services.
steps: Run script as normal user.
Scan error logs and failed units.
Use service-specific scripts for deeper checks.
expected_output: Error log lines and list of failed units.
scripts.rollback: No rollback needed; this script reads status only.
notes: Some logs may require sudo depending on system settings.

scripts.title: Follow live logs of one service
scripts.summary: Stream live journal logs for a selected systemd service.
steps: Run script and provide service name.
Watch live logs and stop with Ctrl+C.
expected_output: Continuous log lines from selected service.
scripts.rollback: No rollback needed; this script reads logs only.
notes: Very useful after restart or config changes.
script_lines: read -rp "Service name for live logs: " SERVICE
echo "Press Ctrl+C to stop log streaming."

scripts.title: Create timestamped tar.gz backup
scripts.summary: Create compressed backup archive from a selected directory.
steps: Enter source directory and backup destination directory.
Script creates timestamped .tar.gz archive.
Test archive listing with tar -tzf.
expected_output: Path to created archive file.
scripts.rollback: No rollback needed; script creates new file only.
notes: Schedule this script with cron/systemd timers for regular backups.
script_lines: read -rp "Source directory to back up: " SRC_DIR

scripts.title: Restore tar.gz backup to destination
scripts.summary: Preview backup content and extract to a chosen target directory.
steps: Enter archive path and destination directory.
Review preview list from archive.
Type yes to confirm extraction.
expected_output: Files extracted to destination directory.
scripts.rollback: Delete extracted files or restore from previous state if destination conflicts.
notes: Restore into an empty temporary path first when possible.

scripts.title: Run system update with dnf
scripts.summary: Check and apply package updates on Fedora/RHEL-family systems using dnf.
steps: Script checks available updates.
Type yes to apply updates.
Reboot if kernel or critical libraries were updated.
expected_output: dnf transaction summary and completion message.
scripts.rollback: Package rollback is not always simple; snapshot/backup before major upgrades.
notes: This script is for dnf-based distributions.
script_lines: read -rp "Install all available updates now? (yes/no): " ANSWER

scripts.title: Basic SSH hardening audit
scripts.summary: Print important SSH server settings and service state.
steps: Run script and review printed SSH settings.
Check if root login and password authentication policies match your standards.
expected_output: Current sshd_config lines for key security directives.
scripts.rollback: No rollback required; script only reads config and service state.
notes: After config edits, validate with sshd -t and restart sshd carefully.

scripts.title: Open common firewall services (ssh/http/https)
scripts.summary: Interactive firewalld helper to open selected common services.
steps: Choose which services to open.
Script applies permanent rules and reloads firewalld.
Validate resulting active zone configuration.
expected_output: firewall-cmd --list-all output with selected services.
scripts.rollback: Remove services using firewall-cmd --permanent --remove-service=<name> and reload.
notes: Opening unnecessary ports increases attack surface.
